questions.question: What is the primary purpose of using Notion Calendar's blocking features?

options.text: To share your calendar details with other team members.
options.explanation: Blocking is primarily for privacy. It prevents others from scheduling over your time without necessarily sharing your specific commitments.

options.text: To automatically move events from your personal calendar to your work calendar.
options.explanation: Blocking simply marks time as busy; it does not move or transfer the actual events between calendars.

options.text: To prevent teammates from scheduling over your events.
options.explanation: Correct. Blocking ensures that specific events or whole calendars are marked as busy, making it impossible for others to book over that time.

options.text: To change the time zone of your events automatically.
options.explanation: Time zone changes are handled separately in the Time Zones settings, not via blocking.

questions.question: How do you initiate the process to block an event on desktop or web?

options.text: Go to Settings > Calendar > Block Event.
options.explanation: Blocking is done directly within the calendar interface using a right-click context menu, not through the main settings menu.

options.text: Right-click an event and select 'Block on calendar'.
options.explanation: Correct. The instructions specify right-clicking an event to access the 'Block on calendar' option.

options.text: Click the 'Eye' icon on the left sidebar to select the target calendar.
options.explanation: The eye icon is used to hide calendars from the sidebar view, not to initiate the blocking process.

options.text: Drag and drop the event into the target calendar.
options.explanation: Blocking does not move the event; it creates a separate 'block' on the target calendar to reserve the time.

questions.question: When setting up a block, what is the difference between selecting 'This event' versus 'All events from {calendar name}'?

options.text: 'This event' creates a block for a single occurrence, while 'All events' sets up auto-blocking for the future.
options.explanation: Correct. 'This event' blocks a specific instance, whereas 'All events' ensures that every future event on that calendar is blocked.

options.text: 'This event' blocks the calendar permanently, while 'All events' only blocks for the current week.
options.explanation: There is no time limit mentioned for auto-blocking; it applies to future events indefinitely until removed.

options.text: 'This event' is only available on desktop, while 'All events' is available on mobile.
options.explanation: Both options are available for desktop and web users as described in the article.

options.text: 'All events' allows you to choose the visibility details, while 'This event' hides them automatically.
options.explanation: Visibility details (like sharing details vs. showing as busy) are chosen in step 4, regardless of which event option is selected in step 3.

questions.question: What does an open lock icon (🔒) next to a calendar indicate regarding the visibility of event details?

options.text: The calendar is currently hidden from the sidebar.
options.explanation: A hidden calendar is indicated by the eye icon, not the lock icon.

options.text: The event details are shared and visible to others on the target calendar.
options.explanation: Correct. An open lock means the event details have been included/shared from the original calendar.

options.text: The auto-blocking feature has been turned off.
options.explanation: Auto-blocking status is not indicated by the lock icon; it is indicated by the selection of 'All events'.

options.text: The block is currently being edited by another user.
options.explanation: There is no indication of collaborative editing status shown by the lock icon in this context.

questions.question: How can you remove a block from a calendar without deleting the original event?

options.text: Right-click the original event and select 'Unblock'.
options.explanation: You right-click the *blocked* event to remove the block, not the original event.

options.text: Right-click the blocked event and unselect the calendar where the block lives.
options.explanation: Correct. This action removes the block on the target calendar while leaving the source event intact.

options.text: Go to Calendar Settings and delete the calendar.
options.explanation: Deleting the calendar would remove all events, not just the block.

options.text: Click the 'Eye' icon on the blocked event.
options.explanation: The eye icon hides the calendar from the sidebar, but does not remove the block.

questions.question: Why might you select 'Include details from the original calendar event' when creating a block?

options.text: To save storage space on your device.
options.explanation: Notion does not use local device storage for calendar data in this manner.

options.text: To allow others to see the specific details (like appointment type) on the target calendar.
options.explanation: Correct. This option shares the details of the event (e.g., 'Doctor's Appointment') on the blocked calendar.

options.text: To hide the block from the target calendar.
options.explanation: The 'Show as busy' option hides the specific details; 'Include details' is the opposite.

options.text: To automatically move the event to the target calendar.
options.explanation: Blocking does not move the event; it only reserves the time slot.

questions.question: How do you turn off auto-blocking for an entire calendar?

options.text: Select 'This event' in the blocking menu.
options.explanation: Selecting 'This event' only blocks a single occurrence, not the entire calendar's future events.

options.text: Right-click a blocked event and unselect the calendar.
options.explanation: Correct. Unblocking all events on that calendar turns off the auto-blocking feature.

options.text: Contact Notion support immediately.
options.explanation: While you can contact support for issues, removing the block is a manual action you can perform yourself.

options.text: Delete the original calendar entirely.
options.explanation: Deleting the source calendar is not the standard way to stop auto-blocking; you simply remove the block.

questions.question: If the lock icon next to a calendar block is closed, what does this mean?

options.text: The event details are hidden and private.
options.explanation: Correct. A closed lock indicates that the specific details of the event are hidden, and only 'busy' status is shown.

options.text: The calendar is currently hidden from the sidebar.
options.explanation: The sidebar visibility is controlled by the eye icon, not the lock icon.

options.text: The auto-blocking feature is broken.
options.explanation: The lock icon indicates privacy settings, not the functionality of the auto-blocking feature itself.

options.text: The block has been successfully removed.
options.explanation: The block is still active; the lock icon simply changes state based on the visibility settings chosen.

questions.question: How can you view a blocked event's details on the target calendar without revealing the original calendar in the sidebar?

options.text: You cannot view the details if the original calendar is hidden.
options.explanation: You can view the details on the target calendar even if the source calendar is hidden, provided the details were included when creating the block.

options.text: Select the eye icon next to the original calendar's name in the left sidebar.
options.explanation: Correct. Hiding the source calendar from the sidebar allows you to focus on the block on the target calendar while keeping the source private.

options.text: Right-click the block and select 'Show details'.
options.explanation: You view the details by looking at the event on the target calendar; there is no separate 'Show details' action for the block itself.

options.text: Delete the original event from your calendar.
options.explanation: Deleting the event would remove the source data, which would eventually remove the block as well.

questions.question: What should you do if you no longer have access to a calendar that is set up for auto-blocking?

options.text: The auto-blocking will automatically stop working.
options.explanation: Auto-blocking persists even if you lose access to the source calendar.

options.text: Contact Notion support via email.
options.explanation: Correct. The article states to reach out to support for this specific issue.

options.text: Unblock all events manually.
options.explanation: While you can unblock events, if you don't have access to the source calendar, you cannot easily manage the blocks until the calendar is restored or support intervenes.

options.text: Create a new account to manage the calendar.
options.explanation: Creating a new account is not the solution to managing access to existing blocks.
